name: chapel-small
summary: An intimate worship space for a household, village, or side-chapel of a larger temple. A single axis from door to altar, a handful of pews or kneeling benches, and a patron's effigy.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: 2:1 — short nave with a clear door-to-altar axis

accent.purpose: altar dais — a step or platform at the far end

primary_palette.note: simple wooden or stone altar with flanking candles
patron saint or deity effigy on the far wall
household font used for daily blessing; a single bench in front
rural folk-faith — a carved idol rather than a deity image

story_prompts: a family chapel — fresh flowers on the altar, a child's carved toy left as offering
a garrison side-chapel — helmets stacked by the kneeling bench, a soldier's prayer in progress
long-neglected — dust on the pews, the altar candle burned down to a puddle
a chapel dedicated to a forbidden or minor saint — signs of careful, quiet maintenance
recently re-consecrated — the scent of incense, new banner still creased from folding

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: warm and intimate (0.35-0.45) — one or two concentrated sources near the altar

anti_patterns: altar always at the far end from the door — the axis is the whole point
never crowd the nave with pillars; this is an intimate room, not a cathedral
seating faces the altar — no benches along side walls
